# The Complete Guide to Customer Feedback Collection for SaaS (ProductLift, March 2026)

## Summary
Comprehensive guide covering all feedback collection channels with signal quality ratings, volume estimates, and management effort. Includes the critical comparison between in-app widgets (passive, high volume, medium signal), feedback boards (passive, medium-high volume, high signal), and other channels (surveys, support tickets, sales calls). The channel comparison table is the single best reference for the collection surface taxonomy guide.

## Key quotations / statistics
- **Channel comparison table:**
  | Channel | Type | Signal Quality | Volume | Effort | Best For |
  |---------|------|----------------|--------|--------|----------|
  | In-app widgets | Passive | Medium | High | Low | Continuous collection from active users |
  | Feedback boards | Passive | High | Medium-High | Low | Feature prioritization with voting signal |
  | Email (forwarding) | Active/Passive | Medium | Low-Medium | Low | Milestone moments |
  | Support tickets | Passive | High (contextual) | High | High | Bug reports, pain points |
  | Sales calls | Active | Very High | Low | High | Buying decisions, objections |
  | NPS/CSAT surveys | Active | Low-Medium | Medium | Low | Trend tracking |
- **Why feedback boards excel:** "The voting mechanism surfaces demand signals that surveys can't replicate. You can sort by Most Voted, Trending, or Recent to see different angles. Because voters are tracked with email, plan type, and MRR, you can segment the signal by customer value."
- **Public vs private board guidance:** "Public boards build transparency and community. They also reduce duplicate submissions because users can see and vote on existing requests. Private boards work better for sensitive B2B feedback or tighter control. Many teams start private and go public once they have a moderation process."
- **Recommended channel stacks by goal:**
  - For roadmap prioritization: Feedback board + in-app widget
  - For churn reduction: Support ticket tagging + NPS survey + churn email
  - For onboarding improvement: In-app widget (triggered at key moments) + customer interviews
  - For idea validation: Customer interviews + feedback board voting data
- **Voting deduplication benefit:** "When 200 people vote for the same feature, that's a fundamentally different signal than 200 separate support tickets about different things. The feature voting mechanism quantifies demand in a way surveys and interviews can't."
- Private-first approach: Start private, go public once moderation process exists.

## Annotations for stinger-forge
- **The channel comparison table is ready-to-use for `guides/01-collection-surface-taxonomy.md`** - copy and adapt.
- The "segment by customer value (MRR, plan type)" point bridges to the RICE scoring guide — feedback boards that show voter MRR are more useful for RICE's Reach component.
- The "private-first, then public" progression is a low-risk path for teams nervous about public transparency.
- The recommended channel stacks by goal (roadmap vs churn vs onboarding) should be the opening decision tree in the collection surface guide.
- The "voting quantifies demand" argument is the core case for feedback boards over generic support tickets in the de-duplication discipline guide.
